title: prunella prunelliformis
intro: Prunella prunelliformis, also known as Selfheal or Heal-all, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small blue flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including wound healing, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Selfheal has been used to treat a range of health conditions, from colds and flu to skin irritations and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-aging properties. Herbal preparations of Selfheal include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin conditions, or internally to promote overall health and well-being.
